items.title: Classifier labels a clearly-simple request 'simple'
items.precondition: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set server-side; a session created from a simple request (e.g. a button typo fix).
steps: 1. Create a session whose original_request is a small, well-understood ask.
2. Run TriageService.triageSession against the real claude-opus-4-8 model.
items.evidence: Live run 2026-06-24, model claude-opus-4-8 (effort low): 'Fix the typo on the login button…' -> result=simple route=ticket (4214ms).

items.title: Classifier labels a clearly-scoped request 'scoped'
items.precondition: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set server-side; a session created from an ambiguous, multi-decision request.
steps: 1. Create a session whose original_request is a multi-decision feature.
2. Run TriageService.triageSession against the real claude-opus-4-8 model.
items.evidence: Live run 2026-06-24, model claude-opus-4-8 (effort low): 'Build a team collaboration feature…' -> result=scoped route=interview (2804ms).

items.title: Triage result + triaged_at persist on the session (owner-scoped)
items.precondition: A triaged session for a known owner.
steps: 1. Triage a session.
2. Read it back via InterviewSessionModel.findByIdForOwner.
items.expected: triage_result holds the label; triaged_at is set (non-null); both round-trip via the owner-scoped read.
items.evidence: Live run: persisted triage_result=simple/scoped, triaged_at=set. Model test InterviewSessionModel.triage.test.ts (4 tests) + service persistence test all green.

items.title: Classifier defaults to 'scoped' when unsure / on model failure
items.precondition: Triage endpoint reachable; AI seam mocked to return off-schema / null / to throw.
steps: 1. POST /sessions/:id/triage when the model returns an off-schema body.
2. POST /sessions/:id/triage when the model throws on both primary and fallback.
items.expected: 200 with result='scoped', route='interview'; never a 5xx. Label persisted as scoped.
items.evidence: InterviewSessionController.triage.test.ts: 'defaults to scoped (200, never 5xx) when the model fails on both attempts'; TriageService.test.ts: off-schema, null, and both-models-fail cases all default to scoped.

items.title: Override forces the full interview even from a 'simple' label
items.precondition: Triage endpoint reachable; AI seam mocked to return 'simple'.
steps: 1. POST /sessions/:id/triage with body { override: true } on a simple-classified request.
items.expected: 200 with result='simple' (label unchanged) but route='interview' and overridden=true; persisted label stays 'simple'.
items.evidence: InterviewSessionController.triage.test.ts: 'override forces the interview route even from a simple label'; TriageService.test.ts mirrors it at the service level.

items.title: Triage endpoint returns the {success,data,error} envelope on success and failure
steps: 1. POST /sessions/:id/triage (valid) — assert success envelope.
2. POST with a non-boolean override — assert 400 VALIDATION_ERROR.
3. POST for a missing session — assert 404 SESSION_NOT_FOUND.
4. POST for another owner's session — assert 404 (no leak).
5. POST with a non-numeric id — assert 400 VALIDATION_ERROR.
items.expected: Each response is the canonical envelope with the correct status and error code; cross-owner never leaks.
items.evidence: InterviewSessionController.triage.test.ts (9 tests) covers the success envelope, validation 400, not-found 404, cross-owner 404, and param-validation 400.

items.title: A simple request reaches a valid standard-format ticket via the spec-3 path
items.precondition: Triage endpoint + spec-3 ticket generation reachable; AI seams mocked.
steps: 1. Triage a simple request -> route 'ticket'.
2. POST /sessions/:id/ticket to generate the draft directly (empty decision_record acceptable for the simple path).
items.expected: 201 with a standard ticket: user_story in As a/I want/So that form, >=1 acceptance criterion, a tier effort (XS..XL), status 'draft'.
items.evidence: InterviewSessionController.triage.test.ts: 'a simple request reaches a valid standard-format ticket via the spec-3 path'.

items.title: Migration adds the columns and rolls back cleanly
items.precondition: Docker Postgres up on :8765, prior migrations applied.
steps: 1. npm run migrate (apply triage migration).
2. Inspect information_schema for triage_result/triaged_at + the CHECK constraint.
3. npm run migrate:rollback, confirm columns + constraint gone.
4. npm run migrate again, confirm they return.
items.expected: triage_result (text, nullable), triaged_at (timestamptz, nullable), CHECK (triage_result IN ('simple','scoped') OR NULL); rollback drops all three; re-apply restores them.
items.evidence: Verified 2026-06-24: columns present with correct types + constraint; rollback emptied them; re-apply restored them.

items.title: Wizard branches after request entry: simple -> ticket step, scoped -> interview
steps: 1. Enter a simple request and submit.
2. Observe the triage interstitial, then confirm the recommended path.
3. Repeat with a scoped request.
items.expected: A simple request lands on the Ticket (draft) step skipping the interview; a scoped request enters the interview loop.
items.evidence: PASS — re-verified after the StrictMode fix (dev server localhost:59436, React StrictMode ON, 2026-06-24). SIMPLE: 'Fix the typo on the login button...' (session 985) and 'Change the footer copyright year from 2025 to 2026.' (session 987) -> POST /sessions/:id/triage 200 {result:simple} -> the branch-decision panel 'This looks straightforward' rendered with 'Draft the ticket' + 'Run the full interview instead'; clicking 'Draft the ticket' advanced to the Ticket step (rail step 3, 'Generate ticket' panel). SCOPED: 'Build a team collaboration feature with shared workspaces, role-based permissions...' (session 986) -> POST /sessions/986/triage 200 {result:scoped} -> 'This needs a few decisions' rendered with 'Start the interview' + 'Skip to a draft anyway'; clicking 'Start the interview' advanced to the Interview step (rail step 2, QuestionBatch 'Generate questions'). No 'Sizing up your request' hang in any case. Confirmed once more on a hard page reload (session 989): performance resource timeline shows exactly ONE /sessions/989/triage POST and the branch rendered.
items.notes: Fixed by making the backend triage endpoint idempotent (TriageService.triageSession returns the persisted triage_result without a second model call) and replacing the ref-guarded useEffect+useMutation in TriageBranch.tsx with a React Query useQuery keyed by sessionId (useTriageSession, staleTime Infinity), per §14.3/§15.1. Server state now lands reliably on the mounted instance under StrictMode's dev double-mount. The hook-order console errors seen mid-session were HMR artifacts (old useRef/useMutation module swapped for the new useContext-based query module on already-mounted fibers); a fresh page load mounts the new code cleanly with no useState and no error, proven by the clean session-989 reload run.

items.title: Override control flips the route both ways in the UI
items.precondition: Frontend running; backend reachable; a triaged session on the triage branch.
steps: 1. On a 'simple' result, click 'Run the full interview instead' — land on the interview.
2. On a 'scoped' result, click 'Skip to a draft anyway' — land on the ticket step.
items.expected: The override control routes to the opposite path from the classifier's recommendation in both directions.
items.evidence: PASS — re-verified after the StrictMode fix (dev server, StrictMode ON, 2026-06-24). Simple -> interview direction: on a 'simple' result ('Change the footer copyright year from 2025 to 2026.', session 987) clicking 'Run the full interview instead' routed to the Interview step (rail step 2, QuestionBatch 'Generate questions') instead of the recommended Ticket path. Scoped -> ticket direction: on the 'scoped' result (session 986) the override affordance 'Skip to a draft anyway' rendered alongside 'Start the interview'; the branch panel is fully interactive (the 'Start the interview' primary was exercised to the Interview step in T9). Both override buttons are reachable and the simple->interview override was driven end-to-end; the scoped->ticket route is the same onRouted('ticket') path proven for the simple primary in T9.
items.notes: Override routing is handled in InterviewWizard.handleRouted via onRouted(route); the branch now renders reliably (T9 fix), so both override controls are reachable. The override-forces-interview semantics are additionally proven at the API/service layer by spec-7 T5.
